mnemonics: CIE Cycle: Collect Insights, Implement, Evaluate
keyPoints: Continuous Internal Evaluation (CIE) is an ongoing assessment method used to monitor student progress throughout a module.
It typically involves a variety of assessment components, such as quizzes, assignments, projects, and presentations, not just a single final exam.
The goal of CIE is to provide regular feedback, encourage consistent learning, and reduce the pressure associated with one-off, high-stakes examinations.
commonMistakes: Confusing CIE with a single mid-term exam; it is a series of assessments.
Underestimating the cumulative impact of smaller CIE components on the final grade.
Procrastinating on CIE tasks because they are 'not the final exam', leading to a poor overall score.
